summary: AI forward-deployed engineer for operational workflows.
editor_note: Enterprise AI is more credible when it stays with the workflow rather than leaving as a consulting handoff. InstaLILY says Lily runs in production across quoting, diagnosis, routing, and sales coverage, with examples from hours to seconds and 98% lower cost to serve. Unlike chat agents, the product is closer to deployed software maintenance. The risk is whether each deployment can scale without heavy services.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes Lily as an AI forward-deployed engineer that learns a business, builds needed software, deploys it, and stays current.
The official site lists operational systems and data sources including SAP, NetSuite, Epicor, Salesforce, HubSpot, Snowflake, inboxes, and PDFs.
The official research post says Lily is live in construction, industrial distribution, logistics, field service, and healthcare operations.

market_context.primary_user: Operations leaders in industrial distribution, construction, logistics, field service, and healthcare operations.
market_context.differentiation: InstaLILY positions Lily as deployed software that keeps adapting to company workflows rather than a chatbot or temporary consulting build.
market_context.why_now: Enterprise AI buyers are shifting from model demos toward production deployment, integration, governance, and maintenance inside existing systems.

risks.claim: The falsifiable risk is whether each customer deployment can remain productized enough to avoid a labor-heavy forward-deployed engineering model.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official product pages, official Series B research post, and funding coverage.
